env.pitch_type: Hybrid natural grass (Kentucky bluegrass / perennial ryegrass sobre base de arena y sistema de drenaje Permavoid instalado sobre hormigon). Cesped cultivado en Denver durante 18 meses y trasladado refrigerado a Houston.
env.notes: Partido jugado en sede neutral (NRG Stadium, Houston, TX). Hora de inicio: 12:00 PM local / 18:00 UTC. El techo retractil estara CERRADO por decision de la FIFA para proteger el cesped y por el calor extremo exterior (indice de calor de hasta 43°C fuera del estadio). Interior climatizado con sistema de AC de 12.000 toneladas. Fuera del estadio: calor extremo con aviso de calor en vigor, temperatura real ~32°C, humedad ~78%, viento del SE a ~13 km/h, probabilidad de lluvia/tormenta del 20% alrededor del mediodía (las tormentas tipicas de Houston llegan por la tarde, 14-17h). El estadio con el techo cerrado aislará las condiciones climaticas exteriores. Aficion holandesa (Oranje) dominante: Fanwalk masivo de ~5.000 aficionados desde Rice University + presencia generalizada de naranja en todo Houston. Aficion sueca tambien organizo marcha de 5.000-7.000 personas desde Helix Park. Estimacion de reparto: ~62% holandeses, ~30% suecos, ~8% neutrales/americanos. El home_support_pct asignado corresponde a Países Bajos como equipo con mayor presencia de aficion. Ruido estimado en 82/100: estadio lleno (72.220), techo cerrado (amplifica el sonido), dos aficiones muy organizadas y vocales, partido de alto voltaje en fase de grupos con implicaciones de clasificacion (Holanda necesitaba ganar tras el 2-2 vs Japon). Sweden venia con 5-1 vs Tunisia. Fuentes: NWS Houston/Galveston, AccuWeather, NBC Sports, KHOU, CW39, Houston Public Media, FIFA.com.

home.key_notes:
FORMACION: 4-3-3 confirmada. Ronald Koeman repite bloque del empate 2-2 ante Japon (14/6), con un cambio obligado: Brobbey (ST) entra por Summerville en la punta; Malen y Gakpo como extremos. Quinten Timber descartado por conmocion cerebral (choque con De Jong vs Japon).

BAJAS/LESIONES CONFIRMADAS: Jurrien Timber (ingle, OUT del torneo, sustituido por Geertruida); Xavi Simons (LCA, OUT del torneo); Quinten Timber (conmocion, OUT vs Suecia).

ALERTA CRITICA - Frenkie de Jong: Koeman confirmo 'signo de interrogacion' por molestia abdominal/zona inferior. Entrenó con el grupo pero su titular es una duda real hasta el calentamiento. Riesgo de lesion elevado (35/100), estado emocional bajo (68/100) por incertidumbre fisica, reduccion de stamina_base (72) y durabilidad (60) reflejan una temporada muy accidentada en Barcelona (solo 21 titularidades, los ultimos 16 partidos en menos de 30 min cada uno).

CONTEXTO DE HOY: Netherlands necesita ganar para no verse en apuros en Grupo F (1 punto, 3ros). Suecia lidera con 3 pts tras golear 5-1 a Tunisia. Presion maxima: derrota dejaria a Oranje en situacion comprometida. Motivaciones elevadas en toda la plantilla menos De Jong (condicion fisica en duda). Brobbey arranca de inicio con alta motivacion (90/100) al tener su primera titularidad en el torneo. Gakpo llega con algo de confianza tocada tras ser neutralizado por Japon (76/100 estado emocional). Van de Ven es el jugador mas rapido del equipo y del torneo (top speed 37.4 km/h, record Premier League). Van Dijk, capitan y lider, con la mejor resistencia a presion del XI (93/100). NRG Stadium Houston, sede neutral con ~70.000 espectadores, temperatura humeda que puede afectar stamina en segunda mitad.

away.key_notes:
Formation: 3-5-2 confirmed by Graham Potter, unchanged from 5-1 Tunisia win. Sweden top Group F after matchday 1. No injuries or suspensions reported — full squad available. Key absences from total squad: Dejan Kulusevski (knee surgery setback, not called up), Emil Holm (pre-tournament injury, replaced by Herman Johansson).

GK Nordfeldt (36, AIK): Oldest player in squad, third-choice experience. Allsvenskan form solid (7.23 avg rating). Slight risk factor due to age vs elite opposition — Netherlands will test him.

CB Lagerbielke (26, SC Braga): Scored in World Cup qualifying playoff vs Poland. Strong in aerial and ball recovery (Europa League: 6.2 recoveries/game). Composed in possession (87.1% pass accuracy).

CB Hien (27, Atalanta): Originally a striker until age 21 — gives him unusual aggression and anticipation for a CB. Reliable Serie A pairing partner. €23.8M valuation reflects genuine quality.

CB Lindelof (31, Aston Villa — captain): 77 caps, former Man Utd. Won Europa League with Aston Villa 2026. Leadership anchor of the backline. Slight drop in top speed vs prime years but reading of the game compensates.

RWB Bernhardsson (27, Holstein Kiel, 2. Bundesliga): Less elite club platform than rest of XI. Energetic but the weakest link offensively. Defensive pressure from Dutch attackers (Gakpo, Summerville) will challenge him.

CAM Nygren (24, Celtic): Stunning debut season — 20 goals across all comps. Plays the number 10 role between midfield and the two strikers. High motivation after historic 5-1 win. First World Cup, riding the wave.

CDM Karlstrom (30, Udinese — club captain): The anchor. Accumulated 6 yellow cards in Serie A 2025-26, so discipline is a real risk under pressure. Covers ground well (27.46 km in 4 qualifying matches). Passing accuracy in qualifying was only 68.75% — may be under pressure vs Dutch midfield quality.

CM Ayari (22, Brighton): Breakout star of the tournament so far. Two stunning long-range goals vs Tunisia. Born to Tunisian/Moroccan parents, chose Sweden. 3 goals and 3 assists in 1,924 Premier League minutes. emotional_state elevated — the confidence of his World Cup debut performance is a game-changer. Top mental attribute currently.

LWB Gudmundsson (27, Leeds United): Suffered cramps vs Tunisia (tracked as mild fatigue/injury flag — injury_risk set higher at 22). 24 senior caps, 1 goal. Important outlet for Sweden's left flank, overlapping behind Isak.

ST Gyokeres (28, Arsenal): Premier League winner 2025-26. 5 goals, 1 assist in 7 international appearances in 2026 season. Hat-trick in qualifying vs Ukraine. 1 goal + 1 assist vs Tunisia, rating 8.7 FotMob. Elite finishing, holds up play, world-class aerial presence for his height. Peak form.

ST Isak (26, Liverpool): 1 goal + 2 assists vs Tunisia, rating 8.6. Comparisons to Thierry Henry in movement. Top recorded speed 32.41 km/h in qualifying (34.1 km/h estimated at full sprint in open play per club data). Returned from broken leg during qualifying — now fully fit. Dangerous in behind, lethal 1v1 vs keepers. The faster and more technical of the two strikers.

Tactical note: Sweden's 3-5-2 is built for quick transitions — compact defensively, explosive on the break through Isak and Gyokeres. Ayari's long-range threat adds a set-piece and open-play dimension. The Dutch struggled defensively vs Japan (conceded 2 in a 2-2 draw) making Sweden's counter-attack system highly dangerous today. Houston heat (mid-30s Celsius expected) benefits Sweden's high-stamina midfield approach.
